title: ONNX: The Universal Bridge
concept: ONNX (Open Neural Network Exchange) is an open format that allows models to be shared and run across different frameworks and hardware platforms.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is latency in production machine learning?

options.text: The time it takes to get a prediction for a single input
The total number of training images processed per second
The memory size of the weight parameters in megabytes

exercises.explain: Latency represents the time delay to process a single request from start to finish.

cards.title: The framework tower of Babel
cards.body: Imagine writing a book in PyTorch, but your publisher only reads TensorFlow, and the printer only understands C++. Translating everything manually is a nightmare. ONNX solves this by acting as a universal translator for AI.

cards.title: What is ONNX?
cards.body: ONNX (Open Neural Network Exchange) defines a common file format and set of operators that allow models to transition seamlessly between frameworks like PyTorch, TensorFlow, and various hardware runtimes.

cards.title: PDF format for neural networks
cards.body: Think of ONNX like a PDF. You can write a document in Microsoft Word, Google Docs, or Pages, but you save it as a PDF so anyone can view it on any device without needing the original app.

cards.title: PyTorch to ONNX Runtime
cards.body: An engineer trains a YOLO detector in PyTorch. Instead of exporting python files, they export the model to a .onnx file and deploy it using ONNX Runtime on a Windows laptop or web browser.

cards.title: Exporting is not always seamless
cards.body: You might believe that any model can be converted to ONNX automatically with one click. In reality, if your model uses custom operations or complex dynamic loops, you may need to write custom exporters.

cards.title: The ONNX Workflow
cards.body: How ONNX connects training and deployment:

left.text:
PyTorch, TensorFlow, Jax
Flexible for writing code
Exports to .onnx file

right.text:
ONNX Runtime, TensorRT
Optimized for execution speed
Runs on CPUs, GPUs, Edge

exercises.explain: ONNX stands for Open Neural Network Exchange, an open format for representing machine learning models.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Using ONNX allows you to run a model trained in PyTorch on a hardware device using ONNX Runtime without having PyTorch installed on that device.
exercises.explain: True. ONNX decouples the training framework from the inference environment, so you only need the runtime to execute it.

exercises.sentence: Saving a model as a universal ___ file allows it to run across different frameworks.
exercises.explain: ONNX files contain the standardized computation graph and weight parameters of the model.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is the main benefit of exporting a model to ONNX?

options.text: Interoperability and deployment flexibility across platforms
Increasing the training speed of the model on raw datasets
Automatically gathering new training labels from users

exercises.explain: Exporting to ONNX makes it easy to run the model on different devices, frameworks, and runtimes.

exercises.prompt: Your team trained a vision model in PyTorch, but the hardware team requires C++ execution on specialized edge CPUs. What is the most standard path?

options.text: Export the PyTorch model to ONNX, then run it using ONNX Runtime in the C++ environment.
Rewrite the entire neural network layers from scratch in pure C++ math libraries.
Install full Python and PyTorch on the tiny edge CPU to run the python training script.
Train a new model in a C++ framework from scratch using the raw datasets.

exercises.explain: ONNX provides a bridge: you export the model once, and use ONNX Runtime C++ APIs to run inference efficiently on the edge CPU.
